metadata.districtModel: five sections; five district slots per section; D13 is the seat of power
sourceLimitations: Street source for Section 5 is missing; preserve hero-visible anchors only and do not invent missing section anchors.

cityDNA.coreFantasy: A titanborn city where Titan Hand Terrace of the Colossus Steps, Border Wall Beacon, Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry, and the five-section district spine turn local identity into functional civic space.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Colossi Prime should feel immediately identifiable through exact landmarks and materials, not through generic fantasy-city shorthand.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Colossi Prime organizes power through Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry, public response through Border Wall Beacon, civic memory through Titan Hand Terrace of the Colossus Steps, and daily life through S1 Fortress / Border Wall Civic Hall, S2 High Residential Civic Hall, S3 Records / Archives Hall, S4 Arena / Training Civic Hall, S5 Star Observatory.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Granite, Bronze, Cast Iron, Arcane Glow, Blue Granite, Cliffside, Terraced Slope, Civic Terraces, Ward Networks, Ritual Geometry, Stone Giant Memory, and exact anchors including Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry, Border Wall Beacon, Titan Hand Terrace of the Colossus Steps, Star Observatory, Manor Court, Training Arena, Fortress / Border Wall Civic Hall, Woven Banners Monument, Watch Bastion Nook, High Residential Civic Hall.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: A titanborn civic culture where architecture, public obligations, roads, landmark approaches, and everyday work must all read as part of the same civilization.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: The city symbol system is built from exact names and visible functions: Titan Hand Terrace of the Colossus Steps, Border Wall Beacon, Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry, and section anchors such as Fortress / Border Wall Civic Hall, High Residential Civic Hall, Records / Archives Hall, Arena / Training Civic Hall, Star Observatory.

canonLocks: Colossi Prime uses city code CPE.
The Wonder is Titan Hand Terrace of the Colossus Steps.
The Beacon is Border Wall Beacon.
The Seat of Power is Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry and occupies District 13.
The city uses five sections with five tactical district slots per section.
The source-backed section spine is: S1 Fortress / Border Wall Civic Hall, S2 High Residential Civic Hall, S3 Records / Archives Hall, S4 Arena / Training Civic Hall, S5 Star Observatory.

rejectedReads: Do not treat Colossi Prime as a generic fantasy settlement with renamed landmarks.

cautionFlags: Keep every named Wonder, Beacon, Seat, section civic hall, major landmark, monument, and minor nook exact when present in sidecars.

Every district asset should remain usable for narrative scenes, tactical board dioramas, and city map backgrounds.

environment.environmentalLogic: The environment should make Colossi Prime's exact civic anchors plausible at street scale: terrain routes people toward section landmarks, lighting clarifies public authority, and water or landscape features remain source-backed rather than decorative filler.

streets.appearance: Streets should show Colossi Prime's exact anchor system at pedestrian scale: S1 Fortress / Border Wall Civic Hall, S2 High Residential Civic Hall, S3 Records / Archives Hall, S4 Arena / Training Civic Hall, S5 Star Observatory. streets.movement: Movement should be readable for tactical-board assets: citizens pass between 25 district slots, section anchors, D13, service streets, and landmark approaches without visual clutter hiding the civic purpose.

smells: worn stone, timber, metal, plaster, or fabric
streets.lighting: Use the package's source-backed civic lighting, daylight/golden-hour reads, lanterns, and beacon glow. Lighting must help identify authority, safety, and landmark function.
streets.weatherEffects: Weather should reinforce the local terrain and materials without overwhelming the city identity or inventing unsupported climate extremes.
streets.dayCycle: Day scenes emphasize errands, trade, records, repairs, rituals, food, and section-level movement.
streets.nightCycle: Night scenes emphasize lantern routes, closed thresholds, guarded landmarks, quiet work, and beacon visibility.

housing.wealthy: Prestige housing clusters near Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry, high terraces, or landmark-facing routes, using the best source-backed materials and controlled views of civic space.
housing.middle: Shop-over-house units, courtyard homes, guild-adjacent apartments, and family rooms near section anchors.
housing.poor: Smaller back-street rooms, work-adjacent housing, shared courtyards, service alleys, patched materials, and dense but still city-specific lived-in detail.
